content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good morning'? {{c1::Bom dia}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good morning' en Portuguese? Bom dia
content.usage: Term: Bom dia | Meaning: Good morning

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good afternoon'? {{c1::Boa tarde}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good afternoon' en Portuguese? Boa tarde
content.usage: Term: Boa tarde | Meaning: Good afternoon

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good evening / night'? {{c1::Boa noite}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good evening / night' en Portuguese? Boa noite
content.usage: Term: Boa noite | Meaning: Good evening / night

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Hello'? {{c1::Olá}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hello' en Portuguese? Olá
content.usage: Term: Olá | Meaning: Hello

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'How are you? / Everything well?'? {{c1::Tudo bem?}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'How are you? / Everything well?' en Portuguese? Tudo bem?
content.usage: Term: Tudo bem? | Meaning: How are you? / Everything well?

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Bye'? {{c1::Tchau}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Bye' en Portuguese? Tchau
content.usage: Term: Tchau | Meaning: Bye

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'See you later'? {{c1::Até logo}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'See you later' en Portuguese? Até logo
content.usage: Term: Até logo | Meaning: See you later

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Hello / Hi' informally? {{c1::Olá}}
content.explanation: Standard friendly greeting used at any time of day.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hola' en portugués? Olá.
content.usage: Term: Olá | Example: Olá, como vais? (Hi, how are you?)

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good evening / Good night'? {{c1::Boa noite}}
content.explanation: Used both upon arriving and departing after dark.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Buenas noches' en portugués? Boa noite.
content.usage: Term: Boa noite | Example: Boa noite, dorme bem. (Good night, sleep well.)

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Goodbye' formally or for long separations? {{c1::Adeus}}
content.explanation: Literally 'to God'. For daily farewells, shorter terms like 'tchau' or 'até logo' are preferred.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Adiós' en portugués? Adeus.
content.usage: Term: Adeus | Dialogue: - Adeus! - Até logo!

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'See you later'? {{c1::Até logo}} or {{c1::Até mais}}.
content.explanation: 'Até logo' is very common when you expect to see the person later that day.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hasta luego' en portugués? Até logo.
content.usage: Term: Até logo | Dialogue: - Vou ao supermercado. - Até logo!

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Please'? {{c1::Por favor}}
content.explanation: The universal and polite way to make requests.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Por favor' en portugués? Por favor.
content.usage: Term: Por favor | Example: Um café, por favor. (A coffee, please.)

content.text: In Portuguese, if you are a male, you say 'Thank you' as '{{c1::Obrigado}}'. If you are a female, you say '{{c2::Obrigada}}'.
content.explanation: The word agrees with the gender of the speaker, not the listener.
content.spanish: En portugués, las gracias concuerdan con el hablante: 'Obrigado' (hombre) o 'Obrigada' (mujer).
content.usage: Male: Obrigado. | Female: Obrigada.

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'You're welcome'? {{c1::De nada}}
content.explanation: Standard response to thank you. Can also hear 'não há de quê' (it's nothing).
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'De nada' en portugués? De nada.
content.usage: Dialogue: - Obrigado! - De nada.

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you ask someone their name? {{c1::Como se chama?}} and the response is '{{c2::Chamo-me}} [Name]'.
content.explanation: Standard pronominal verb construction in European Portuguese. In Brazil, 'Como você se chama?' or 'Qual o seu nome?' is more common.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo te llamas?' en portugués? 'Como se chama?' y la respuesta es 'Chamo-me...'.
content.usage: Dialogue: - Como se chama? - Chamo-me Jesus.

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Nice to meet you' upon introductions? {{c1::Muito prazer}} or {{c1::Prazer}}.
content.explanation: 'Muito prazer' means 'much pleasure'.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Mucho gusto' en portugués? Muito prazer.
content.usage: Dialogue: - Chamo-me Maria. - Muito prazer, eu sou o Carlos.

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you ask 'How are you?' politely? {{c1::Como está?}} or informally {{c2::Tudo bem?}}.
content.explanation: 'Tudo bem?' (All good?) is extremely popular in all contexts. 'Como está?' is the standard verb structure.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo está / Todo bien?' en portugués? Como está? / Tudo bem?.
content.usage: Dialogue: - Olá, tudo bem? - Tudo bem, obrigado.

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'See you tomorrow'? {{c1::Até amanhã}}
content.explanation: Common parting phrase for people who meet daily.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hasta mañana' en portugués? Até amanhã.
content.usage: Term: Até amanhã | Dialogue: - Adeus! - Até amanhã!

content.text: In Portuguese, the most common informal way to say 'Bye / Goodbye' is '{{c1::Tchau}}'.
content.explanation: Derived from Italian 'Ciao'. Extremely common in daily conversational Portuguese.
content.spanish: En portugués, la forma informal más común de decir 'Adiós' es 'Tchau'.
content.usage: Term: Tchau | Example: Tchau, até logo! (Bye, see you later!)

content.text: In Portuguese, how do you ask 'How are you doing? / How's it going?' in a friendly way? {{c1::Como vai?}}
content.explanation: A very common alternative to 'Como está?' and 'Tudo bem?'.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo te va?' en portugués? Como vai?.
content.usage: Term: Como vai? | Dialogue: - Olá, como vai? - Tudo ótimo, e contigo?

content.scenario: Portuguese A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'I am'? {{c1::Eu sou / estou}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'I am' en Portuguese? Eu sou / estou
content.usage: Term: Eu sou / estou | Meaning: I am

content.scenario: Portuguese A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'You are'? {{c1::Tu és / estás}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'You are' en Portuguese? Tu és / estás
content.usage: Term: Tu és / estás | Meaning: You are

content.scenario: Portuguese A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'You are (standard/Brazil)'? {{c1::Você é / está}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'You are (standard/Brazil)' en Portuguese? Você é / está
content.usage: Term: Você é / está | Meaning: You are (standard/Brazil)

content.scenario: Portuguese A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'We are'? {{c1::Nós somos / estamos}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'We are' en Portuguese? Nós somos / estamos
content.usage: Term: Nós somos / estamos | Meaning: We are

content.scenario: Portuguese A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'They are'? {{c1::Eles são / estão}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'They are' en Portuguese? Eles são / estão
content.usage: Term: Eles são / estão | Meaning: They are

content.text: In Portuguese, the subject pronoun for 'I' is '{{c1::eu}}' and for 'you (singular informal)' is '{{c2::tu}}'.
content.explanation: European Portuguese regularly uses 'tu' for close friends/peers. Brazil mostly replaces 'tu' with 'você'.
content.spanish: En portugués, los pronombres para 'yo' y 'tú' son 'eu' y 'tu'.
content.usage: Examples: Eu sou espanhol. (I am Spanish.) | Tu és estudante? (Are you a student?)

content.text: In Portuguese, the third-person singular pronouns for 'he' and 'she' are {{c1::ele}} and {{c2::ela}}.
content.explanation: They correspond directly to 'él' and 'ella' in Spanish.
content.spanish: En portugués, los pronombres para 'él' y 'ella' son 'ele' y 'ela'.
content.usage: Examples: Ele é médico. (He is a doctor.) | Ela é enfermeira. (She is a nurse.)

content.text: In Portuguese, the subject pronoun for 'we' is {{c1::nós}}.
content.explanation: Conversational Portuguese (especially in Brazil) often replaces 'nós' with 'a gente' (conjugated in third-person singular, e.g., 'a gente é').
content.spanish: En portugués, 'nosotros' se dice 'nós'. Coloquialmente se usa 'a gente'.
content.usage: Example: Nós somos amigos. (We are friends.) | Colloquial: A gente é amigo.

content.text: In Portuguese, the plural pronouns for 'they' (masc/mixed) is {{c1::eles}} and 'they' (fem) is {{c2::elas}}.
content.explanation: They follow direct gender agreement rules.
content.spanish: En portugués, 'ellos' se dice 'eles' y 'ellas' se dice 'elas'.
content.usage: Examples: Eles são engenheiros. (They are engineers.) | Elas são professoras. (They are teachers - fem.)

content.text: In Portuguese, the verb for permanent identity 'to be' is '{{c1::ser}}'. Conjugate 'ser' for 'eu' and 'tu': eu {{c2::sou}}, tu {{c3::és}}.
content.explanation: Ser is used for characteristics like nationality, profession, permanent traits, and origin.
content.spanish: El verbo 'ser' en portugués se conjuga para yo y tú como: eu sou, tu és.
content.usage: Examples: Eu sou do Porto. (I am from Porto.) | Tu és simpático. (You are nice.)

content.text: Conjugate the verb 'ser' (to be) in the present tense for 'ele/ela' (he/she) and 'nós' (we): ele/ela {{c1::é}}, nós {{c2::somos}}.
content.explanation: Note the acute accent on 'é' (is). Without it, 'e' means 'and'.
content.spanish: Conjuga 'ser' en presente para 'él/ella' y 'nosotros': ele/ela é, nós somos.
content.usage: Examples: Ele é arquiteto. (He is an architect.) | Nós somos felizes. (We are happy.)

content.text: Conjugate the verb 'ser' (to be) in the present tense for 'eles/elas' (they): eles/elas {{c1::são}}.
content.explanation: Notice the nasal ending '-ão'.
content.spanish: Conjuga 'ser' en presente para 'ellos/ellas': eles/elas são.
content.usage: Example: Elas são estudantes. (They are students.)

content.text: In Portuguese, the verb for temporary states 'to be' is '{{c1::estar}}'. Conjugate 'estar' for 'eu' and 'tu': eu {{c2::estou}}, tu {{c3::estás}}.
content.explanation: Estar is used for location, health, temporary moods, or current weather.
content.spanish: El verbo 'estar' en portugués se conjuga para yo y tú como: eu estou, tu estás.
content.usage: Examples: Eu estou cansado. (I am tired.) | Tu estás em Lisboa? (Are you in Lisbon?)

content.text: Conjugate the verb 'estar' (to be) in the present tense for 'ele/ela' (he/she) and 'nós' (we): ele/ela {{c1::está}}, nós {{c2::estamos}}.
content.explanation: Estar conjugations carry accents on terminal vowels.
content.spanish: Conjuga 'estar' en presente para 'él/ella' y 'nosotros': ele/ela está, nós estamos.
content.usage: Examples: Como está ele? (How is he?) | Nós estamos no hotel. (We are at the hotel.)

content.text: Conjugate the verb 'estar' (to be) in the present tense for 'eles/elas' (they): eles/elas {{c1::estão}}.
content.explanation: Carries the nasal diphthong '-ão'.
content.spanish: Conjuga 'estar' en presente para 'ellos/ellas': eles/elas estão.
content.usage: Example: Eles estão com pressa. (They are in a hurry.)

content.text: In Portuguese, the pronoun '{{c1::você}}' is formal in Portugal but informal in Brazil, and it conjugates with the {{c2::third-person singular}} form of the verb.
content.explanation: In Portugal, 'você' is polite but some find it slightly cold; name + verb in 3rd person is preferred for polite address. In Brazil, 'você' is the default 'you'.
content.spanish: En portugués, 'você' equivale a 'tú' (Brasil) o 'usted' (Portugal). Se conjuga en tercera persona singular (e.g. você é).
content.usage: Example: Você é de onde? (Where are you from?)

content.text: To negate a sentence in Portuguese, you place the word '{{c1::não}}' before the verb. Example: I am not tired = {{c2::Não estou cansado}}.
content.explanation: Direct and simple negation identical to Spanish 'no'.
content.spanish: Para negar una oración en portugués, se pone 'não' antes del verbo. 'Não estou cansado' = No estoy cansado.
content.usage: Example: Ela não é daqui. (She is not from here.)

content.text: In Portuguese, prepositions regularly contract with personal pronouns. For example, 'de + ele' becomes '{{c1::dele}}' (his/of him) and 'de + ela' becomes '{{c2::dela}}' (her/of her).
content.explanation: Required in Portuguese grammar, unlike Spanish where 'de él' remains separated.
content.spanish: En portugués, 'de + ele/ela' se contrae obligatoriamente como 'dele' (de él) y 'dela' (de ella).
content.usage: Example: O livro é dele. (The book is his.) | A casa é dela. (The house is hers.)

content.text: In Portuguese, 'em + ele' contracts to '{{c1::nele}}' (in him/it) and 'em + ela' contracts to '{{c2::nela}}' (in her/it).
content.explanation: Combines preposition 'em' (in/on/at) with the pronoun.
content.spanish: La preposición 'em' se contrae con 'ele/ela' como 'nele' y 'nela'.
content.usage: Example: Eu acredito nele. (I believe in him.)

content.text: Just like Spanish, yes/no questions in Portuguese are formed purely through {{c1::intonation}} without changing the word order.
content.explanation: A statement becomes a question simply by raising the pitch of your voice at the end.
content.spanish: Al igual que el español, en portugués las preguntas de sí/no se indican únicamente mediante la entonación.
content.usage: Statement: Tu és o Jesus. | Question: Tu és o Jesus?
